2606.14122.title: Beyond Perplexity: UTF-8 Validity in Byte-aware Language Models
2606.14122.paper_summary: Byte-level tokenization enables language models to handle any Unicode input, but models can generate invalid UTF-8 sequences when encountering rare or unseen characters. We investigate the relationship between training scale and UTF-8 generation reliability with a 355M parameter model trained on 80B tokens from a balanced multilingual corpus of English, Japanese, Korean, and Chinese. We introduce multiple evaluation protocols that isolate UTF-8 structural validity from language modeling. UTF-8 validity convergence lags perplexity by a roughly a factor of two: perplexity stabilizes after 2.1B tokens, but UTF-8 validity requires 4.2B tokens. In context-free generation, rare characters achieve higher structural validity than common characters, suggesting over-specialization of frequent character representations. Through experiments, we observed that reliable UTF-8 generation is a distinct capability requiring evaluation beyond perplexity.
2606.14122.paper_summary_zh: 字節級的標記化使語言模型能夠處理任何 Unicode 輸入，但當遇到罕見或未見過的字符時，模型可能會生成無效的 UTF-8 序列。我們調查了訓練規模與 UTF-8 生成可靠性之間的關係，使用一個 355M 參數的模型，該模型在來自平衡多語言語料庫的 80B 令牌上進行訓練，語言包括英語、日語、韓語和中文。我們引入了多個評估協議，以隔離 UTF-8 結構有效性與語言建模之間的關係。UTF-8 有效性收斂的速度大約比困惑度慢兩倍：困惑度在 2.1B 令牌後穩定，但 UTF-8 有效性需要 4.2B 令牌。在無上下文生成中，罕見字符的結構有效性高於常見字符，這表明頻繁字符表示的過度專門化。通過實驗，我們觀察到可靠的 UTF-8 生成是一種獨特的能力，需要超越困惑度的評估。
2606.14122.author: Sangwhan Moon et.al.
2606.14122.authors: Sangwhan Moon, Daisuke Oba, Youmi Ma, Tatsuya Hiraoka, Naoaki Okazaki
